name: Task
description: A specific piece of work related to project operations that does not involve code changes

name: Bug
description: An unexpected problem or behavior (corresponds to the fix type in Conventional Commits)

name: Feature
description: A request, idea, or new functionality (corresponds to the feat type in Conventional Commits)

name: Improvement
description: An enhancement that improves the maintainability or performance of existing code without changing its external behavior (corresponds to the refactor, style, and perf types in Conventional Commits).

name: Epic
description: A high-level initiative that groups related sub-issues.

name: Maintenance
description: Changes that affect configuration files, documentation, build systems, deployment automation, CI/CD workflows, or other maintenance tasks that don’t affect code behavior (corresponds to the chore, docs, build, and ci types in Conventional Commits).

name: Design
description: Work related to creating, updating, or refining the visual and user experience aspects of the product.  Does not involve code changes.

name: Test
description: Additions, updates, or improvements to automated tests (corresponds to the test type in Conventional Commits).
